content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Numbers on Whiteboard". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/problemset/problem/1430/C
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. The first line of each test case contains an integer n, the number of elements on the whiteboard. The second line contains n space-separated integers, the numbers on the whiteboard.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum number that can be obtained by changing at most one number on the whiteboard.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: The approach is to find the maximum number that can be obtained by changing at most one number on the whiteboard. We can achieve this by iterating over all possible numbers and checking if changing the current number to the maximum number would result in a larger number.

code.Python3:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_num = 0
    for i in range(n):
        temp = a[i]
        a[i] = 9
        num = 0
        for j in range(n):
            num = num * 10 + a[j]
        if num > max_num:
            max_num = num
        a[i] = temp
    print(max_num)
